Class Server

java.lang.Object
networking.Server
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable
public class Server extends Object implements Runnable
Handles the local networking between game clients. Stores data for the two threads ensuring the most up to date information is being sent. It also handles the creation of Server.ReadWrite objects that act as the two thread to move data between the server and its two maximum allowed clients. The server (S) communications follow a strict path for Player 1 (P1) and Player 2 (P2). P1 -> S -> P2 -> S -> P1. This path repeats until the game is complete or the server shuts down.
Author:
Matt Lagier

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    private class 
    Server.ReadWrite
    The ReadWrite class utilizes the socket connections made in acceptConnection() to push data between the server and client.

    • checkBothThreadsWait

      private boolean checkBothThreadsWait()
      Returns a boolean on the status of both thread#Wait booleans, used for the releaseBothThreads() method.
      Returns:
      returns a boolean if both threads are currently waiting.

    • releaseBothThreads

      private void releaseBothThreads() throws InterruptedException
      At the start of a game, each players client sends a copy of their play object to the server. In order to make sure the server sends back a copy of a non-null object, I needed a way to pause the threads until both of them we're ready. This forces each thread to get stuck into a loop that does nothing until both threads switch the thread#Wait boolean. This prevents the server from sending a null type to a player if there was an asynchronous load time between the two clients. For instance if player 1 joined 10 seconds before player 2, player 1 may receive a null type when the thread pulls the data from the server, instead of a player object. My other attempts at looping in the thread didn't work out and this was the best option I could come up with.
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if there is an error when sleeping

    • makeReadWriteP1Wait

      public void makeReadWriteP1Wait() throws InterruptedException
      Force server thread 1 to wait, used for timing of server updates between the players.
      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if there is an interruption.

    • makeReadWriteP2Wait

      public void makeReadWriteP2Wait() throws InterruptedException

      Throws:
      InterruptedException - if there is an interruption.

      public void makeReadWriteP1Notify() throws InterruptedException

      InterruptedException - if there is an interruption.

      public void makeReadWriteP2Notify() throws InterruptedException

    • setPlayer1Arg

      public void setPlayer1Arg(Player one)

    • setPlayer2Arg

      public void setPlayer2Arg(Player two)

    • updateServerP1Thread

      public void updateServerP1Thread()
      Update the server with thread 1s copy of the two player objects. This allows threads to pull the most current data from the server since the threads cannot talk to each other.

    • updateServerP2Thread

      public void updateServerP2Thread()
      Update the server with thread 2s copy of the two player objects. This allows threads to pull the most current data from the server since the threads cannot talk to each other.

    • acceptConnection

      public void acceptConnection() throws ClassNotFoundException, InterruptedException
      Throws:
      ClassNotFoundException
      InterruptedException

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      Static method to start the server when the "Host server" button is clicked in the multiplayer menu. The server can also be launched separately from eclipse if you want to see the print commands.
      Parameters:
      args - not used.

    • run

      public void run()
      Server starts the actual connection process in a way that allows for multithreading.
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable
